params.text:
The primary survey: DR ABC

When you reach a casualty, work through a quick, ordered check to find and treat anything immediately life-threatening. Remember it as DR ABC:

  • D – Danger: before you approach, check the area is safe for you, the casualty and others. Remove the danger or the casualty only if it is safe to do so.
  • R – Response: gently shake the shoulders and ask loudly, "Can you hear me?" See if they respond.
  • A – Airway: open the airway by tilting the head back with one hand on the forehead and lifting the chin with two fingers.
  • B – Breathing: look, listen and feel for normal breathing for no more than 10 seconds.
  • C – Circulation: once breathing is confirmed, check for and control any severe bleeding.

What the check tells you

If the casualty is unresponsive and not breathing normally, call 999 or 112 and start CPR straight away, sending someone for a defibrillator (AED). If they are unresponsive but breathing normally, place them in the recovery position and call for help. Working through DR ABC in order means you always deal with the biggest threat to life first.

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap to complete the DR ABC primary survey.
params.textField: First check for *Danger* to keep yourself safe, then check the casualty's *Response* by speaking to them. Open the *Airway* by tilting the head back, then look, listen and feel for *Breathing*. Finally, check the *Circulation* by looking for severe bleeding.
